title: buildah is creating broken filesystems between layers when symlinks overwrite existing symlinks on the same layer
body:
Tested with:

- podman desktop on macos: 5.2.5
- buildah in eks: buildah version 1.38.0 (image-spec 1.1.0, runtime-spec 1.2.0)

## Examples

I have 3 container files (broken-1, broken-2, and working).

The broken-1 file tries to install the newer libpq (libpq.so.5.17) on top of a previously installed libpq (libpq.so.5.15). This fails only when using buildah. It does not fail when built with docker or with kaniko.

The broken-2 file tries to remove libpq in the same RUN statement as where it tries to install the new libpq. Again this fails only when using buildah. It does not fail when built with docker or kaniko.

The working file separates the remove and install into two separate RUN statements / layers.

In the broken containers you'll see that the symlinks are incorrect in the final image.

### broken-1

```
# Dockerfile.broken-1
FROM public.ecr.aws/docker/library/python:3.9.20-bookworm

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# bookworm ships with postgres 15 so we use the ppa to get >= 16
RUN set -e \
    && echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/pgdg.gpg] http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt bookworm-pgdg main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/pgdg.list \
    && mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyrings \
    && wget --quiet -O - https://www.postgresql.org/media/keys/ACCC4CF8.asc > /tmp/pgdg.asc \
    && gpg --no-default-keyring --keyring /tmp/pgdg.gpg --import /tmp/pgdg.asc \
    && gpg --no-default-keyring --keyring /tmp/pgdg.gpg --export > /etc/apt/keyrings/pgdg.gpg \
    && rm -rf /tmp/pgdg* \
    && apt-get update

RUN apt-get install -y libpq-dev postgresql-client && ls -lah /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libpq*
RUN ls -lah /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libpq*
```

### broken-2

```
# Dockerfile.broken-2
FROM public.ecr.aws/docker/library/python:3.9.20-bookworm

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# bookworm ships with postgres 15 so we use the ppa to get >= 16
RUN set -e \
    && echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/pgdg.gpg] http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt bookworm-pgdg main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/pgdg.list \
    && mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyrings \
    && wget --quiet -O - https://www.postgresql.org/media/keys/ACCC4CF8.asc > /tmp/pgdg.asc \
    && gpg --no-default-keyring --keyring /tmp/pgdg.gpg --import /tmp/pgdg.asc \
    && gpg --no-default-keyring --keyring /tmp/pgdg.gpg --export > /etc/apt/keyrings/pgdg.gpg \
    && rm -rf /tmp/pgdg* \
    && apt-get update

RUN set -e \
    && apt-get remove -y libpq5 libpq-dev \
    && apt-get install -y libpq-dev postgresql-client \
    && ls -lah /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libpq*
RUN ls -lah /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libpq*
```

### working

```
# Dockerfile.working
FROM public.ecr.aws/docker/library/python:3.9.20-bookworm

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# bookworm ships with postgres 15 so we use the ppa to get >= 16
RUN set -e \
    && echo "deb [signed-by=/etc/apt/keyrings/pgdg.gpg] http://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt bookworm-pgdg main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/pgdg.list \
    && mkdir -p /etc/apt/keyrings \
    && wget --quiet -O - https://www.postgresql.org/media/keys/ACCC4CF8.asc > /tmp/pgdg.asc \
    && gpg --no-default-keyring --keyring /tmp/pgdg.gpg --import /tmp/pgdg.asc \
    && gpg --no-default-keyring --keyring /tmp/pgdg.gpg --export > /etc/apt/keyrings/pgdg.gpg \
    && rm -rf /tmp/pgdg* \
    && apt-get update

RUN apt-get remove -y libpq-dev libpq5
RUN apt-get install -y libpq-dev postgresql-client
RUN ls -lah /lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libpq*
```

## Analysis

All 3 of these docker files work fine on docker and on kaniko (rootless) when building an image. I have tested combinations of the default settings as well as `BUILDAH_ISOLATION=chroot` and `STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs` and I'm still seeing the same issue.

Please note that within the layer itself the filesystem looks correct. But when I start a new layer then you will see the issue. So I suspect it is something to do potentially with symlinks between layers and how they are being tracked.

I'd consider this a critical bug.

comments.body:
I have reduced the example that shows the issue down to this (Note, running on ARM64 using Podman desktop on macOS):

```
FROM public.ecr.aws/docker/library/python:3.9.20-bookworm

RUN cp -pr /lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libpq.so.5.15 /lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libpq.so.test
RUN stat /lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libpq.so.5.15
RUN ls -lah /lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libpq* 
RUN cd /lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/ && ln -fs libpq.so.test libpq.so && touch -h -t 202411191436.12 libpq.so && ls -lah /lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libpq*
RUN ls -lah /lib/aarch64-linux-gnu/libpq* 
```

It seems that if the symlink metadata does not change (and the touch here sets the date to match the original file) it will fail:


Alright, was able to reduce it further and changed the base image to make sure it's not related:

```
FROM ubuntu:latest

RUN touch file1.ext && touch -h -t 198804071307.58 file1.ext && cp -pr file1.ext file1.alt
RUN ln -s file1.ext file2.sym && touch -h -t 198804071307.58 file2.sym
RUN ls -lah file*
RUN ln -s file1.alt file2.sym.new && touch -h -t 198804071307.58 file2.sym.new && mv file2.sym.new file2.sym && ls -lah file*
RUN ls -lah file*
```

Seems to work for me with buildah 1.38.0 and podman 5.3.1 on my f40 system
@Luap99 have you tried my original examples using apt?
@Luap99 are you setting `export STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs`? That is required if running buildah within an existing container system such as containerd/podman/docker.

I have not tried using vfs or the original reproducer, I will try that later. Your original report seemed to indicate that it happens with the default which I assumed to be overlay.

It would definitely make sense if this only happens with a single storage driver as they all have their own diff implementation AFAIK.
In EKS (k8s) I'm using STORAGE_DRIVER=vfs and on macOS I'm using the default for podman desktop which I did not think was vfs but I guess I'm not really sure and it happens in those 2 envs.

I checked on macOS and it is using the overlay driver, not vfs.

```
$ podman info --format '{{ .Store.GraphDriverName }}'
overlay
```

To be more clear with the original report, my tests work the same in the following environments:

- macOS podman desktop 5.2.5 with overlay.
- EKS with  buildah 1.38.0 with vfs (with and without chroot). This is using kernel 6.1.97-104.177.amzn2023.x86_64, k8s v1.30.2-eks-1552ad0, containerd://1.7.11.

macOS:

```
❯ podman version

Version:      5.2.5
API Version:  5.2.5
Go Version:   go1.22.7
Built:        Thu Oct 17 18:00:00 2024
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64
```

It seems to work correctly with overlayfs as rootless user but fails when run as root. With vfs it fails in both cases.
@nalind @giuseppe ideas?

```
[root@i-07b40eac73d14cb76 testing]# podman info --format '{{ .Store.GraphDriverName }}'
```

Just in case this is important, `uname -a` information:

```
# uname -a
Linux i-07b40eac73d14cb76.aws.delta.test.scs.internal 5.14.0-427.47.1.el9_4.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon Nov 25 07:17:14 EST 2024 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

that depends from the naive differ, that doesn't account for the symlink target:

https://github.com/containers/storage/blob/main/pkg/archive/changes.go#L331-L346
